label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the text again to understand why the two men cut their clothes. The user is asking for the reason they cut their clothes. From the text: "But my clothes stuck to me like a heavy weight. I could hardly stay above the surface." This shows that the main character's clothes were making it hard to swim because they were heavy and clinging to his body. Counsel saw this and asked, "Shall I cut them?" So he was trying to help by removing the clothes. He then cuts the clothes from top to bottom and takes them off quickly while the main character swims for both of them. The main character does the same for Counsel afterward. The reason they cut the clothes is that the clothes were a burden, making it difficult to stay afloat. By removing the clothes, they could swim more easily. The options given are: (A) Because no people helped them to take off their clothes. (B) Because they couldn't take off their clothes on the sea. (C) Because they wanted to get rid of their clothes quickly. (D) Because they wanted to use their clothes as lifebuoys. Option C seems correct because they wanted to remove (get rid of) their clothes quickly to make it easier to swim. The clothes were a hindrance, so they cut them off quickly to reduce the weight and difficulty in swimming. Option A is incorrect because it doesn't mention that no one was helping. Actually, Counsel was helping the main character. Option B is incorrect because they could take off the clothes, but they needed to do it quickly in the water. The problem wasn't the ability to take them off but the difficulty of doing so in the situation. Option D is incorrect
